title: Cross-sandbox relay API and fleet management
description: Phase 4 core: Build host relay API on port 9090 for cross-sandbox communication. Includes: (1) Express/Node relay service with policy-checked forwarding between sandbox gateways, (2) fleet dashboard improvements showing all sandboxes at a glance with health/status aggregation, (3) channel-to-sandbox routing rules editor so different channels can target different sandboxes. File area: api/, ui/src/app/page.tsx (fleet dashboard), ui/src/app/channels/. Acceptance: relay service starts on :9090, sandbox A can send messages to sandbox B via relay, fleet dashboard shows multi-sandbox overview, channel routing configurable per-channel.
closeReason: Cross-sandbox relay API and fleet management completed. 3 builders dispatched: (1) fleet-ui-builder delivered relay API on :9090, fleet dashboard with 4-stat grid + health bar + relay summary, api.ts types; (2) channel-routing-builder delivered multi-instance channel storage with auto-migration and routing UI; (3) relay-api-builder was redundant. Two branches sent for merge: fleet-ui-builder (primary) and channel-routing-builder. Known conflict on channels/page.tsx — channel-routing-builder version is primary, needs RelayRoutesEditor from fleet-ui-builder merged in.

title: RBAC, enterprise auth, and audit enhancements
description: Phase 5 enterprise auth: (1) RBAC model with admin/operator/viewer roles — middleware in api/lib/auth.js, role checks on routes, (2) UI role-aware rendering — hide/disable actions based on role, (3) OCSF audit log enhancements — richer event types, export capability, retention settings, (4) SAML/OIDC SSO stubs — configuration page, redirect flow skeleton. File area: api/lib/auth.js, api/routes/auth.js, ui/src/app/login/, ui/src/app/audit/, ui/src/app/settings/, ui/src/components/auth-guard.tsx. Acceptance: 3 roles enforced server-side, UI adapts to role, audit page shows filterable events with export, SSO config page exists.
closeReason: RBAC, enterprise auth, and audit enhancements implemented across 2 builders (11 files, 2032 insertions). Backend: 3-tier RBAC (admin/operator/viewer), user store, requireRole middleware, role-tiered route mounting, OCSF audit classification with CSV/JSON export and retention config, SAML/OIDC SSO stubs. Frontend: UserContext with role propagation, role-gated sidebar with badge, enhanced audit page with 10+ event types and actor filter, SSO settings section, user management UI. Both builders merge_ready sent.

title: UI production polish and missing features
description: Final polish pass: (1) Policy comparison/diff viewer — compare policies across sandboxes side-by-side, (2) Usage metering dashboard — track inference calls, token usage, cost estimates per sandbox, (3) Responsive design pass — ensure all 12 pages work on tablet/mobile, (4) Error states and loading skeletons — replace empty catch blocks with user-facing error messages, add skeleton loaders. File area: ui/src/app/, ui/src/components/. Acceptance: policy diff viewer functional, metering page shows usage data, all pages responsive, no silent failures in UI.
closeReason: All 4 deliverables complete: (1) Responsive sidebar with mobile drawer/hamburger menu, (2) Usage metering page deriving data from audit log, (3) Error states replacing all empty catch blocks with user-facing banners + retry, (4) Loading skeletons on all pages using Skeleton component. Policy diff viewer also added as bonus. 4 builders dispatched, all passed self-review.

Store credentials securely (bcrypt hash in ~/.diffract/credentials.json, mode 600). 2) Login page: standard username/password form. On success, set httpOnly session cookie (existing cookie pattern). 3) Remove all token-based auth from api/lib/auth.js and api/routes/auth.js. 4) Update UI login page at ui/src/app/login/ to show either setup form or login form based on GET /api/auth/status (returns {setupRequired: true/false}). 5) Keep requireAuth middleware but validate against stored bcrypt hash instead of token. Added GET /api/auth/status and POST /api/auth/setup routes. UI login page dynamically shows setup or login form based on setupRequired.

This requires: 1) Update Caddy config generation in api/routes/gateway-routes.js: add a new route handle /sandboxes/*/diffract_chat/* that proxies to the sandbox gateway port (same as existing /agent/<name>/ routes but with new path pattern and strip prefix). Remove the old token-based flow since we now use username/password auth. 3) Update deploy/caddy/Caddyfile template to include the new route pattern. 4) On VPS: git pull, rebuild UI, sync Caddy config (POST /api/gateway-routes/sync), restart API+UI. title: fix: bake Chromium into sandbox Dockerfile, remove runtime playwright install
description:
Onboard hangs forever on 'Installing Chromium via Playwright'. Root cause: runtime install runs inside sandbox whose deny-by-default policy blocks cdn.playwright.dev; runCapture has no timeout so execSync hangs; pipe through 'openshell sandbox connect' leaves zombie ssh-proxy children.

Fix:
1. cli/Dockerfile — install Playwright + Chromium during image build (network is available at build time, outside sandbox policy). Add after the npm install -g openclaw step, while still USER root:
   RUN npm install -g playwright@latest \
       && npx playwright install chromium \
       && npx playwright install-deps chromium \
       && CHROME=$(find /root/.cache/ms-playwright -name chrome-headless-shell -type f | head -1) \
       && CHROME_DIR=$(dirname $CHROME) \
       && cp -r $CHROME_DIR /opt/chromium-headless \
       && chmod -R 755 /opt/chromium-headless
   Use headless shell path /opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell.

2. cli/bin/lib/onboard.js — rewrite installBrowser() (lines 724-776) to verify-only:
   - Check /opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell exists via sandboxExec('test -x ...')
   - If present: call configureBrowser() with that path
   - If missing: print warning, do NOT attempt download, continue (non-fatal)
   - Remove all 'npx playwright install' calls and the /root/.cache find logic
   - Rename step label from 'Installing browser' to 'Configuring browser'

3. cli/bin/lib/runner.js — add timeout pass-through to runCapture (defense in depth):
   execSync(cmd, { ..., timeout: opts.timeout }). Let caller opt in.

Acceptance:
- diffract onboard completes past the browser step without hanging even if sandbox network fully blocked
- Chromium binary is usable from the sandbox user (chmod 755)
- openclaw.json browser config updated with correct executablePath
- Existing sandboxes without chromium baked in still proceed with a warning, not a hang
closeReason: Merged overstory/onboard-chromium-builder/Diffraction-1aa2

title: fix: missing UI deps in dashboard/page.tsx and policies/page.tsx breaking ui build
description:
ui npm run build fails due to missing imports in:
- ui/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx
- ui/src/app/policies/page.tsx

Missing modules reported by tsc:
- lucide-react (icon library, needs to be added to ui/package.json)
- @/components/ui/card
- @/components/ui/badge
- @/components/ui/button
- @/components/ui/skeleton
- @/lib/use-gateway

Root cause: pages were written assuming shadcn/ui components and a use-gateway hook that were never added to this repo.

Fix approach:
1. Check ui/src/components/ for existing primitives — the repo may use a different UI lib (Tailwind + plain components). Look at working pages like ui/src/app/page.tsx and ui/src/components/log-viewer.tsx for the house style.
2. Either:
   a) Install shadcn/ui components (npx shadcn@latest add card badge button skeleton) + add lucide-react to ui/package.json, OR
   b) Rewrite the two pages to use existing primitives already in ui/src/components/ui/ (whichever is consistent with the rest of ui/src/app/*)
3. Create @/lib/use-gateway hook if not present — it likely needs to wrap the existing gateway websocket client in ui/src/lib/api.ts.
4. Verify: cd ui && npm run build → zero errors.

Acceptance:
- ui/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx compiles
- ui/src/app/policies/page.tsx compiles
- ui npm run build succeeds end-to-end
- No regression to other pages
closeReason: Merged overstory/ui-deps-builder/Diffraction-e4cb

title: fix: sandbox delete UI shows stale state, no verification of actual removal
description:
ui/src/app/sandboxes/page.tsx:27-35 fires DELETE /api/sandboxes/:name, ignores the {deleted: boolean} response body, and immediately refetches. Actual k8s pod teardown is async (phase Deleting → gone takes 30s+), so the row stays visible until the 5s polling interval eventually catches up. If delete fails server-side the user sees no error.

Fix:
1. ui/src/lib/api.ts deleteSandbox already returns {deleted: boolean}. No change needed.
2. ui/src/app/sandboxes/page.tsx handleDelete:
   - Check the response: if !result.deleted, alert and return.
   - Mark the sandbox as pending-delete in local state (add a Set<string> 'deleting' to state) so the row shows a 'Deleting...' indicator and the delete button disabled.
   - Poll every 2s (up to ~60s) calling listSandboxes(); when the name disappears from the list, remove from the deleting set and refresh.
   - On timeout, alert 'Delete still pending — refresh to check'.
3. Render: rows whose name is in the deleting set get opacity-60 + spinner + 'Deleting' label where the age column normally sits; delete button disabled.

Acceptance:
- Delete button triggers immediate visual feedback (row dims, spinner)
- Row disappears only when sandbox actually gone from backend list
- Failed delete shows error
- 'cd ui && npm run build' clean
- 'npx tsc --noEmit -p ui/tsconfig.json' clean

File scope (narrow): ui/src/app/sandboxes/page.tsx only. Do not touch api.ts or other pages.
closeReason: Merged overstory/sandbox-delete-ui-builder-v2/Diffraction-e024 (bcb0408)

   c) sandbox-delete UX (e024): verify sandbox delete shows pending state + polls every 15s (can simulate by creating a throwaway sandbox then deleting it, or just inspect the compiled JS for the 15000 literal)
6. Report HTTP status for https://srv1534809.hstgr.cloud/, /dashboard, /policies, /sandboxes

Do NOT:
- re-onboard / destroy my-assistant sandbox (chromium fix only applies to fresh builds — report that a manual re-onboard is needed but don't do it)

title: fix: POST /api/sandboxes broken — wrong repo path, invalid provider mapping, missing envs, no status feedback
description:
The UI 'Create Sandbox' button silently fails because api/routes/sandboxes.js:36-74 has multiple bugs. POST /api/sandboxes fires a detached onboard process, returns 202 immediately, and the onboard dies instantly with no feedback to the UI.

Bugs:
1. Wrong repo path (line 52): 'cd /root/diffract' — real path on VPS is /root/.diffract/repo. cd fails, subshell exits, spawn silently dies.
2. Invalid provider mapping (line 40): UI sends provider='nvidia'|'openai'|'anthropic'|'gemini'. cli/bin/lib/onboard.js:338 getNonInteractiveProvider() only accepts 'cloud'|'ollama'|'vllm'|'nim'. Any cloud provider name → process.exit(1).
3. Missing required env vars: NVIDIA_API_KEY (needed for cloud provider), DIFFRACT_DOMAIN (needed for Caddy allowedOrigins). These live in /root/.env on VPS.
4. Policy presets ignored: UI sends spec.policy_presets[], backend never reads it. onboard.js:1116 expects DIFFRACTION_POLICY_MODE=custom and DIFFRACTION_POLICY_PRESETS=comma,list.
5. Fire-and-forget: no way for UI to discover onboard failure. The spawn is detached with stdio ignore — if it dies, the sandbox detail page polls getSandbox forever and never learns.
6. No concurrency lock: two POSTs in quick succession race.

Fix plan (single file: api/routes/sandboxes.js):

A. POST / handler:
   - Correct path: cd /root/.diffract/repo (not /root/diffract)
   - Map UI provider → onboard provider: any of nvidia/openai/anthropic/gemini → 'cloud'; 'ollama' → 'ollama'; 'vllm' → 'vllm'; 'nim' → 'nim'. Keep the cloud-provider type as a separate spec field for later (stored in sandbox settings after onboard completes) if needed; for now just collapse to 'cloud'.
   - Inject env vars from process.env: NVIDIA_API_KEY, DIFFRACT_DOMAIN. Fall back to reading /root/.env if not in process env. Do NOT log the key values.
   - If spec.policy_presets is a non-empty array, add DIFFRACTION_POLICY_MODE=custom and DIFFRACTION_POLICY_PRESETS=<comma-join>. Otherwise let onboard default to 'suggested'.
   - Simple in-memory lock: const activeOnboards = new Map<name, {startedAt, logFile, pid}>. Reject 409 if name is already active.
   - Still fire detached (onboard takes 3-5 min), but record the child PID into the lock.

B. New GET /:name/onboard-status endpoint:
   - Reads the tail of /tmp/diffract-onboard-<name>.log (last 100 lines) via fs.readFile.
   - Returns { active: bool, exitCode: number|null, startedAt, elapsedMs, tail: string[] }.
   - If the lock has a pid, check if the process is alive via process.kill(pid, 0) (no-op signal). If alive → active:true. If not alive → check log tail for success markers ('Onboard complete' or 'provisioning failed') and report exitCode accordingly, then remove from lock.

C. UI side (ui/src/app/sandboxes/[name]/page.tsx — OUT OF SCOPE for this fix, note in report only): the detail page should poll the new onboard-status endpoint during the 'provisioning' phase to surface errors. File a follow-up issue for it.

Acceptance:
- POST /api/sandboxes with {name, spec:{provider:'nvidia', model:'nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b'}} on VPS successfully starts onboard and returns 202.
- The spawned child process actually stays alive past 2 seconds (tail the log file to verify).
- GET /api/sandboxes/<name>/onboard-status returns the tail and active state.
- Sending the same name twice while active returns 409.
- node -c api/routes/sandboxes.js clean.
- No regression to list/get/delete routes.

File scope (NARROW): api/routes/sandboxes.js only. Do NOT touch grpc-client, UI files, or cli/. Do NOT add dependencies.
closeReason: Merged POST /api/sandboxes fixes

title: fix(onboard): skip browser step when chromium missing or env flag set
description: onboard step 6 installBrowser() in cli/bin/lib/onboard.js hangs on VPS even with verify-only logic — the runCapture → openshell sandbox connect → test -x call can stall with no timeout. Fix: (1) honor DIFFRACTION_SKIP_BROWSER=1 env var to skip step entirely and return early. (2) add timeout: 10000 to the runCapture call so it cannot hang indefinitely. (3) on timeout or 'no' result, log warning and return without throwing (already non-fatal per caller). File scope: cli/bin/lib/onboard.js only. Lines 724-748. Run node -c cli/bin/lib/onboard.js after edit.
closeReason: Merged: DIFFRACTION_SKIP_BROWSER env var + 10s timeout

title: fix(onboard): skip browser step by default
description: Make browser step default-skip in cli/bin/lib/onboard.js installBrowser() at line 724. Invert DIFFRACTION_SKIP_BROWSER logic: skip by default unless DIFFRACTION_ENABLE_BROWSER=1 is set. Replace the existing env check (lines 727-730) with: if (process.env.DIFFRACTION_ENABLE_BROWSER !== '1') { console.log('  ✓ Browser step skipped (set DIFFRACTION_ENABLE_BROWSER=1 to enable)'); return; }. File scope: cli/bin/lib/onboard.js only. Verify with node -c cli/bin/lib/onboard.js.
closeReason: Merged default-skip browser

title: fix(onboard): gateway startup hangs because openshell sandbox connect SSH never returns
description: Step 7 setupOpenclaw in cli/bin/lib/onboard.js hangs at 'Starting OpenClaw gateway inside sandbox...' because 'openshell sandbox connect' uses ssh -tt which keeps the pty session open forever after the backgrounded nohup gateway runs. Fix: (1) wrap the gateway-start run() call at line 1057 with a short timeout so the ssh session is killed after command is delivered — use child_process spawn with timeout, or shell wrap with 'timeout 5 ...' prefix. (2) same treatment for the config-sync run() at line 1045. (3) after backgrounding, the sandbox connect should exit on its own when stdin closes — add an explicit 'exit' line to the piped script so ssh closes cleanly. File scope: cli/bin/lib/onboard.js only, inside setupOpenclaw. Verify node -c cli/bin/lib/onboard.js.
closeReason: Merged: exit+timeout 10s on sandbox connect

title: fix(api): gateway_healthy always false — wrong status string
description: api/routes/health.js line 24 checks health.status === 'SERVING' but the gRPC Health RPC (proto/diffract.proto) returns ServiceStatus enum with values SERVICE_STATUS_HEALTHY / SERVICE_STATUS_DEGRADED / SERVICE_STATUS_UNHEALTHY. Curl to /api/health already shows the correct value (SERVICE_STATUS_HEALTHY). Fix: change line 24 to 'gateway_healthy = health.status === "SERVICE_STATUS_HEALTHY";'. File scope: api/routes/health.js only.
closeReason: Merged SERVICE_STATUS_HEALTHY fix

title: feat(ui): collect NVIDIA API key in sandbox creation form
description: Sandbox creation form must collect NVIDIA_API_KEY from user instead of reading from /root/.env on VPS. Non-interactive onboard requires NVIDIA_API_KEY env var for cloud inference (cli/bin/lib/onboard.js line 946). Changes: (1) ui/src/app/sandboxes/new/page.tsx — add a required password-type input field for 'NVIDIA API key' (placeholder 'nvapi-...'), include in spec as nvidia_api_key, validate non-empty before submit, masked input. (2) api/routes/sandboxes.js POST / — accept spec.nvidia_api_key, inject into child env as NVIDIA_API_KEY (overriding any /root/.env fallback), return 400 if missing. File scope: ui/src/app/sandboxes/new/page.tsx + api/routes/sandboxes.js. Verify node -c api/routes/sandboxes.js and cd ui && npm run build (if time permits).
closeReason: Merged NVIDIA API key form field

title: Sandbox detail: show provisioning state during onboard
description:
When user clicks Create Sandbox, the form posts to /api/sandboxes (which spawns 'diffract onboard --non-interactive', returns 202) and navigates to /sandboxes/[name]. The detail page at ui/src/app/sandboxes/[name]/page.tsx immediately calls getSandbox(name), which fails with 'gRPC GetSandbox: sandbox not found' because onboard takes 3-5 min to actually create the sandbox.

Fix: when getSandbox 404s, fall back to GET /api/sandboxes/:name/onboard-status (already exists in api/routes/sandboxes.js — returns {active, exitCode, startedAt, elapsedMs, tail}). While active or sandbox not yet found, render a provisioning panel showing: status ('Provisioning...'), elapsed time, and the last ~20 lines of the log tail. Poll every 3s. Once getSandbox succeeds, switch to the normal detail view. If onboard-status returns active=false AND exitCode!=0 AND sandbox still 404, show an error state with the log tail.

Files: ui/src/app/sandboxes/[name]/page.tsx (and may add a small ui/src/lib/api helper getOnboardStatus). Quality gates: cd ui && npm run build must succeed.
closeReason: Detail page handles provisioning state via /onboard-status polling

title: install.sh: pin OPENSHELL_VERSION on correct side of pipe
description:
install.sh line 154 has a bug:

OPENSHELL_VERSION="0.0.21" curl -LsSf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/main/install.sh | sh

The env var is set only for the curl process — bash does NOT propagate it to the piped 'sh'. Result: the OpenShell installer falls back to 'latest' (currently 0.0.22 which has port forward regression AND k3s gateway init failures).

Also: the OpenShell release tags use a 'v' prefix (v0.0.21), so the version string must be 'v0.0.21' not '0.0.21'.

Fix: move the env var to the sh side of the pipe, with the v prefix:

curl -LsSf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/main/install.sh | OPENSHELL_VERSION=v0.0.21 sh

Files: install.sh (line ~154). Quality gates: bash -n install.sh.
closeReason: OPENSHELL_VERSION pinned correctly on sh side with v prefix

title: Full survey of Reference/ upstreams (OpenShell, OpenClaw, NemoClaw, guides)
description:
Deep-read the Reference/ directory at /Users/hrushi/Downloads/Desktop offline/vibe coding/AI BIZ/Diffraction/Reference/. Four subtrees:
- Reference/OpenShell/ — NVIDIA OpenShell, Rust workspace (crates/, proto/, deploy/, python/, docs/)
- Reference/OpenClaw/ — OpenClaw, TypeScript pnpm monorepo (src/, apps/, packages/, docs/, channels)
- Reference/NemoClaw/ — NVIDIA NemoClaw reference stack (bin/, nemoclaw/, nemoclaw-blueprint/, k8s/, scripts/, docs/)
- Reference/guides/ — setup guides

Goal: produce a deep mental model of how each upstream works internally so future Diffract work can reference it without re-exploring. Survey each crate/package: purpose, public API, dependencies on siblings, notable patterns. Read the architecture docs, proto files, main entry points, install scripts, blueprints, and skill definitions. Skip giant generated files (package-lock.json, CHANGELOG.md >500KB, .secrets.baseline, uv.lock) — note their existence only.

Deliverables: record findings to mulch across relevant domains (architecture, deployment, openshell, openclaw, nemoclaw) — each record should capture a concrete convention, pattern, decision, or reference usable by future agents. Aim for 15-30 mulch records total, not a single dump. Use --classification foundational for stable upstream conventions, observational for anything unverified.

Do NOT modify any source files. Read-only research task. Quality gate: ml status should show new records in the expected domains.
closeReason: Scout surveyed Reference/ upstreams, recorded 32 mulch insights across architecture/deployment/security

title: Replace /tmp log-file onboard job model with SQLite job table + SSE
description:
Current: api/routes/sandboxes.js spawns 'diffract onboard' as detached bash, tracks PID in in-memory activeOnboards Map, tails /tmp/diffract-onboard-<name>.log and infers status by grepping for 'complete'/'Error:'. State lost on API restart. Concurrent onboards collide.

Replace with: SQLite job table at ~/.diffract/jobs.db with columns (id, sandbox_name, status, started_at, finished_at, exit_code, log_path). Worker pool with concurrency=1 per host. cli/bin/lib/onboard.js emits structured step events ({step, status, ts}) written to job row. UI subscribes to /api/events SSE stream instead of polling /onboard-status every 3s.

Files: api/lib/jobs.js (new), api/routes/sandboxes.js, api/routes/events.js (new), cli/bin/lib/onboard.js, ui/src/app/sandboxes/[name]/page.tsx, ui/src/lib/api.ts.

title: mTLS cert hot-reload in api/lib/grpc-client.js
description:
Current: gRPC client loads ca.crt/tls.crt/tls.key once at module load. When openshell gateway destroy+start regenerates certs (e.g. during onboard step 2), the API keeps using stale certs and every gRPC call fails with 'certificate signature failure'. Workaround today is manual API restart.

Fix: watch /root/.config/openshell/gateways/diffract/mtls/ with fs.watch (recursive); on change, rebuild grpc.credentials.createSsl() and swap the active client. Add a debounce (1s) to coalesce burst writes during cert rotation.

Files: api/lib/grpc-client.js. Quality gates: existing api tests pass; manual test: destroy gateway → start → confirm /api/sandboxes works without API restart.
closeReason: Added fs.watch cert watcher to api/lib/grpc-client.js with 1s debounce. Rebuilds gRPC mTLS client automatically when ca.crt/tls.key/tls.crt change. Eliminates manual API restart after gateway cert rotation.

title: Rewrite cli/bin/lib/onboard.js as explicit state machine
description:
Current: 7 imperative steps (preflight, gateway, sandbox, provider_selection, inference, openclaw, policies) with hand-rolled resumability via onboard-session.json. Bugs keep leaking: stale session blocks retry, browser step hangs, openshell sandbox connect PTY hang on step 7, NVIDIA_API_KEY check after spawning all the prep work. Resume logic is scattered.

Rewrite using XState (or a small custom FSM if XState is too heavy). Each state declares: entry action, exit action, timeout, onError transition, idempotency contract. Resume logic falls out of the machine — load persisted state, transition into the saved state. Each step gets a unit test covering happy path + timeout + error rollback.

Files: cli/bin/lib/onboard.js, cli/bin/lib/onboard-machine.js (new), cli/test/onboard-machine.test.js (new). Quality gates: all existing onboard tests pass; new state-machine tests cover each step's contracts.
closeReason: Rewrote onboard.js orchestration as OnboardMachine FSM. New file onboard-machine.js with step lifecycle (execute/timeout/onError/nonFatal/restoreCtx), SessionAdapter interface, withTimeout helper, and onEvent structured event emission. 25 tests in onboard-machine.test.js. Fixed pre-existing onboard.test.js binary name assertion.

title: Replace Express API bridge with Fastify + Zod schemas
description:
Current: api/ is hand-rolled Express, no schema validation, hardcoded shell-escaping in sandboxes.js, execSync everywhere, no shared types with UI. Routes silently accept any body. Shell-injection surface in restart-gateway endpoint (sandbox name interpolated into bash).

Rewrite with Fastify + Zod schemas at every route boundary. Define request/response types in api/schemas/ and re-export to ui/src/lib/api.ts so UI gets typed clients. Eliminate execSync; talk to gateway via existing grpc-client. For sandbox-internal commands, spawn with array args (no shell), never string interpolation.

Files: api/server.js, api/routes/*.js, api/schemas/*.ts (new), ui/src/lib/api.ts. Quality gates: api tests rewritten + pass; cd ui && npm run build.

title: Caddy admin API config patches instead of Caddyfile regeneration
description:
Current: api/routes/gateway-routes.js POST /sync rewrites the entire /etc/caddy/Caddyfile from a template every time a sandbox is created/deleted. Race-prone: two concurrent sandbox creates can stomp each other. Requires file write + systemctl reload.

Replace with Caddy's admin API at http://127.0.0.1:2019/config/. PATCH the routes array to add/remove individual sandbox routes. No file writes. No reload. Atomic.

Files: api/routes/gateway-routes.js, deploy/caddy/Caddyfile (enable admin endpoint, lock to localhost). Quality gates: api tests cover add+remove route; manual test on VPS confirms two concurrent creates both succeed.

title: Replace install.sh with Rust diffract-installer binary
description:
Current: install.sh is 8 imperative bash steps with no rollback, swallows errors with 2>/dev/null||true, has had multiple bugs (env var on wrong side of pipe, nvm PATH, cgroup config). Hard to test, hard to debug remotely. Assumes apt — won't work on RHEL/Alpine.

Replace with a static Rust binary diffract-installer (new crate or extension of crates/diffract-bootstrap) shipped via GitHub releases. Single curl → static binary → runs all install steps with proper error handling, dry-run mode, idempotent re-runs, structured logs, rollback on failure.

Files: crates/diffract-installer/ (new), install.sh (replace body with single curl + exec), .github/workflows/release-installer.yml (new). Quality gates: cargo test, cargo clippy, integration test on fresh Ubuntu Docker container.

title: OIDC SSO auth replacing bcrypt-in-JSON credentials
description:
Current: /root/.diffract/credentials.json holds bcrypt password hash. No SSO, no MFA, no per-user RBAC enforcement at gRPC layer (UI checks but the API can be hit directly). Single admin user.

Add OIDC support: tenants configure their IdP (Keycloak/Auth0/Okta/Google Workspace). UI redirects to IdP for login, receives signed token, presents to API on every request. API verifies token signature + claims. Per-sandbox RBAC enforced at gRPC interceptor, not just UI guards. Keep bcrypt as fallback for first-run/no-IdP installs.

Files: api/lib/auth.js, api/middleware/oidc.js (new), ui/src/app/login/page.tsx, ui/src/lib/auth.ts, diffract.yaml.example (oidc section). Quality gates: api tests cover token verify happy path + expired + bad signature; manual test against a Keycloak Docker container.

title: End-to-end Playwright test: UI create sandbox → ready
description:
Current: cli/test/ has unit tests, but the full flow (UI form → POST /api/sandboxes → onboard.js → openshell → sandbox up → chat iframe loads) has zero coverage. Every bug we hit this week (cert reload, NVIDIA key missing, browser hang, gateway PTY hang, gRPC SERVING vs SERVICE_STATUS_HEALTHY, Caddy missing route) would have been caught by one e2e test.

Add Playwright suite that:
1. Spins up a fresh Diffract install in a Docker container
2. Logs in via UI
3. Clicks Create Sandbox, fills form with NVIDIA key, submits
4. Waits for detail page to show 'ready' (not provisioning)
5. Verifies chat iframe loads /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat
6. Tears down

Files: e2e/playwright.config.ts (new), e2e/sandbox-create.spec.ts (new), e2e/Dockerfile (new), .github/workflows/e2e.yml (new). Quality gate: full suite green in CI.

title: Federation: central control plane → N remote OpenShell nodes
description:
Current: one Diffract install = one host. Enterprises with multiple teams either run multiple installs (no central view) or cram everything on one VM (no isolation). Today's gRPC client is hardcoded to localhost:8080.

Architecture: split control plane (UI + API + auth + audit aggregator) from data plane (OpenShell + sandboxes). Each remote node registers with the control plane via signed JWT + mTLS. Sandbox scheduler picks node by load and policy constraints. Audit events stream from each node to control plane and merge into a single OCSF stream.

This is multi-week work. Pre-requisites: issues for OIDC (#7), Fastify rewrite (#4), and SQLite job table (#1) should land first because they're load-bearing.

Files: crates/diffract-controlplane/ (new), crates/diffract-node-agent/ (new), api/, proto/diffract.proto (federation messages), ui/src/app/nodes/ (new). Quality gates: cargo test, e2e against 2-node cluster.
